data.content:
Plan (2026-03-31):
1. Reproduce and isolate the source-of-truth mismatch in the history UI. The current session card label is built from session-level fields captured at session start ( via  in  and ). For the reported entry ( ,  ), the raw response already carries provider-specific metadata under , so the history row is not using the same identity source shown in response metadata.
2. Add an entry-level response identity extraction step in the history parser. Extend the parsed response entry shape in  and  so response rows can carry a provider/model display source derived from response metadata instead of only inheriting the session-start label. The parser should inspect parsed response payloads for provider/model metadata first, including the OpenRouter-backed tool/step payload shape seen in the failing log, and keep the existing session-level fields only as a fallback.
3. Centralize display-label selection so history rows and session headers use the same precedence rules. Introduce or reuse a small helper that prefers response metadata identity, then normalized provider/model fields, then existing session label fields. Reuse existing agent/model formatting helpers where possible (, ) so OpenRouter/OpenCode-style model paths render consistently and do not get relabeled as local Codex/OpenAI identities.
4. Update the history UI surfaces to consume the new source. The affected render path is the conversation/session card and response row (, , and any shared label helper). Scope is limited to the displayed agent label only; timestamps, prompt numbering, workflow badges, and other history behavior must remain unchanged.
5. Add regression coverage. Extend parser/history tests ( and/or ) with a fixture that contains a response entry carrying OpenRouter metadata and assert that the returned history entry/session exposes the OpenRouter-derived label. Add a render-level assertion for the session/response UI if needed so the visible label matches metadata-derived identity instead of a hardcoded or session-start Codex label.
6. Validation strategy for implementation: run targeted history tests first, then at minimum 
 RUN  v4.0.18 /Users/cartine/foolery

 ✓ |unit| src/lib/__tests__/agent-history-summaries.test.ts (7 tests) 37ms
 ✓ |unit| src/lib/__tests__/agent-history-coverage-roots.test.ts (5 tests) 41ms
 ✓ |unit| src/lib/__tests__/agent-history-coverage.test.ts (10 tests) 42ms
 ✓ |unit| src/components/__tests__/agent-history-view-debug-integration.test.ts (7 tests) 12ms
 ✓ |unit| src/lib/__tests__/agent-history-beat-detail-render.test.ts (1 test) 11ms

 Test Files  6 passed (6)
      Tests  36 passed (36)
   Start at  10:53:41
   Duration  458ms (transform 574ms, setup 0ms, import 1.10s, tests 176ms, environment 0ms) or the closest targeted Vitest selection for the touched suites. If implementation touches shared formatting helpers, expand to the relevant agent-identity/model-label tests as well.

Complexity: low to moderate. The main risk is selecting the wrong precedence when both session-start identity and response metadata are present. The implementation should prefer the exact response metadata source used by the response detail view, with a strict fallback to existing session fields so older logs without metadata keep rendering.
